user_query:
I need bilingual signage ready for our upcoming Summit Peaks Outdoor Festival. Please inspect the configuration and i18n files in input/ to determine which English signage keys are required and whether Spanish is enabled, then produce two outputs:

1) Create output/i18n/es.json containing Spanish translations for exactly the keys listed under i18n.signage_keys_required in input/site_config.yaml. Use the English source text from input/i18n/en.json and map it to Spanish using the exact case and accents from input/glossary.csv. If a required key is missing in en.json, set its value to "REVIEW: MISSING_ENGLISH_TEXT". If the English text exists but has no glossary mapping, set its value to "REVIEW: <English text>" (include the English text after the colon). Do not include any keys that are not listed in i18n.signage_keys_required.

2) Prepare output/meeting_notes.md as concise meeting notes with action items. Include:
- Context: festival name and participant_count_estimate (from site_config.yaml) and a note that bilingual signage is needed.
- Languages enabled (from i18n.languages_enabled) and whether Spanish is enabled.
- Status summary with counts: total required keys, how many translated in es.json, how many flagged for review, and a bullet list of keys under each category (translated vs review) with reasons for review ("Missing English source string" or "Missing glossary mapping").
- Action items assigned to owners.content_ops and owners.signage_ops (from site_config.yaml). Content Ops should provide or confirm Spanish translations for all review items, respecting constraints.translation_deadline_days. Signage Ops should confirm print files will use output/i18n/es.json after translations are resolved. Reference output file paths explicitly in the action items.

Use only the provided files; do not invent translations beyond the glossary. The two deliverables must be reproducible from the inputs: es.json built from required keys and the glossary, and meeting_notes.md that summarizes findings and assigns owners and deadlines based on site_config.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
